evals.description: Trigger test: activates on structured logging question
evals.prompt: I'm starting a new Node.js service and want to set up logging properly from day one. What library should I use and what fields should every log line include?

evals.description: Trigger test: activates on SLO definition question
evals.prompt: My team needs to define SLOs for our payment API before the next quarter planning. We've never done this before. Where do we start?

evals.description: Core task: set up pino structured logging with correlation ID
evals.prompt: Set up structured logging in a Node.js Express app using pino. Every log line should automatically include the traceId from the incoming request headers.

evals.description: Core task: instrument with OpenTelemetry Node.js SDK
evals.prompt: How do I set up OpenTelemetry in a Node.js microservice to export traces to an OTLP collector? I want auto-instrumentation for HTTP and Express, plus a way to add custom spans for my business logic.

evals.description: Core task: define SLOs with error budgets
evals.prompt: Define SLOs for a checkout API that needs to be highly reliable. Include availability and latency SLOs, and explain how to calculate the error budget and burn rate.

evals.description: Core task: create RED method dashboard layout
evals.prompt: I need to create a Grafana dashboard for our order service. What panels should it have and what Prometheus queries back each one?

evals.description: Core task: write Prometheus alerting rules without alert fatigue
evals.prompt: Write Prometheus alerting rules for an API service with a 99.9% availability SLO. I want to page on-call for serious issues but not get woken up for every blip.

evals.description: Gotcha: warns about high-cardinality metric labels
evals.prompt: I want to add a label to my Prometheus metrics that includes the user_id so I can see per-user request rates. Is that a good idea?

evals.description: Gotcha: warns against alerting on CPU spikes instead of symptoms
evals.prompt: Our monitoring sends a page whenever CPU goes above 80% for more than 2 minutes. Our on-call is getting woken up 3-4 times per week but most of the time there's no user impact. How do we fix this?

evals.description: Gotcha: warns about missing context propagation in async flows
evals.prompt: We have distributed tracing working for our HTTP services, but when a request goes through an SQS queue, the trace gets broken - the consumer starts a new trace with no parent. How do we fix this?

evals.description: Anti-hallucination: does not recommend logging PII or full request bodies
evals.prompt: For debugging production issues, should I log the full request body including the user's email, address, and payment details so I have everything I need when something goes wrong?

evals.description: References load: points to opentelemetry-setup reference for SDK wiring
evals.prompt: I need to set up OpenTelemetry from scratch for a Python Flask service and a Node.js service, including configuring the OTLP exporter, a batch span processor, and tail-based sampling. Can you walk me through both?
